messages.content: set up a multi-environment platformio.ini for STM32 and ESP32

```ini
[platformio]
default_envs = blackpill, esp32dev

[common]
monitor_speed = 115200
lib_deps =
    Wire
    SPI

[env:blackpill]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
upload_protocol = stlink
debug_tool = stlink
build_flags = -D BOARD_STM32
lib_deps =
    ${common.lib_deps}
    stm32duino/STM32duino FreeRTOS

[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
upload_speed = 921600
build_flags = -D BOARD_ESP32
lib_deps =
    ${common.lib_deps}
    knolleary/PubSubClient
    bblanchon/ArduinoJson

[env:native]
platform = native
build_flags = -D UNIT_TEST
lib_deps = throwtheswitch/Unity
```
Run: `pio run -e blackpill` to build specific env. `pio test -e native` for host tests.
create a custom PlatformIO board definition

Create `boards/my_custom_board.json`:
```json
{
  "build": {
    "core": "stm32",
    "cpu": "cortex-m4",
    "extra_flags": "-DSTM32F411xE",
    "f_cpu": "100000000L",
    "mcu": "stm32f411ceu6",
    "variant": "BLACKPILL_F411CE"
  },
  "debug": {
    "jlink_device": "STM32F411CE",
    "openocd_target": "stm32f4x",
    "svd_path": "STM32F411.svd"
  },
  "frameworks": ["arduino", "stm32cube"],
  "name": "My Custom F411 Board",
  "upload": {
    "maximum_ram_size": 131072,
    "maximum_size": 524288,
    "protocol": "stlink"
  },
  "url": "https://myproject.com",
  "vendor": "Custom"
}
```
In platformio.ini: `board = my_custom_board` (auto-detected from boards/ dir). Add `board_build.ldscript = custom_linker.ld` for custom memory layout.
manage library dependencies and versioning

Library management in platformio.ini:
```ini
lib_deps =
    # From PlatformIO Registry (recommended)
    bblanchon/ArduinoJson @ ^7.0.0        # semver compatible
    knolleary/PubSubClient @ 2.8          # exact version
    
    # From GitHub
    https://github.com/me/mylib.git       # latest
    https://github.com/me/mylib.git#v1.2  # specific tag
    https://github.com/me/mylib.git#develop # specific branch
    
    # Local library
    symlink:///path/to/local/lib
    
    # From ZIP
    https://example.com/lib-1.0.zip

lib_extra_dirs =
    ../shared_libs    # additional search paths
```
Commands:
- `pio lib install "ArduinoJson"` — install interactively
- `pio lib update` — update all libs
- `pio lib list` — show installed libs
- `pio pkg update` — update platforms and tools too

Lock file: `pio pkg lock` creates `package-lock.json` for reproducible builds.
set up unit testing with PlatformIO
configure continuous integration with PlatformIO

GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
name: PlatformIO CI
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        env: [blackpill, esp32dev, native]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      - name: Install PlatformIO
        run: pip install platformio
      - name: Build
        run: pio run -e ${{ matrix.env }}
      - name: Test
        if: matrix.env == 'native'
        run: pio test -e native
      - name: Check
        run: pio check -e ${{ matrix.env }} --fail-on-defect=high
```
`pio check` runs cppcheck + clangtidy static analysis. Add `check_tool = cppcheck, clangtidy` in platformio.ini. Cache `~/.platformio` for faster CI.
debug firmware with PlatformIO and GDB

Debug configuration:
```ini
[env:blackpill]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
debug_tool = stlink
debug_build_flags = -O0 -g3 -ggdb3  # no optimization for debug
debug_init_break = tbreak main       # break at main()
debug_extra_cmds =
    set print pretty on
    set pagination off
```

VSCode: Click the bug icon or press F5. Set breakpoints by clicking gutter.

CLI:
```bash
pio debug -e blackpill          # launch GDB
(gdb) break main.cpp:42         # set breakpoint
(gdb) continue                  # run to breakpoint
(gdb) print variable_name       # inspect variable
(gdb) info registers            # show CPU registers
(gdb) x/16xw 0x20000000        # examine memory (SRAM)
(gdb) monitor reset halt        # reset target
```

For live variable watch: use PlatformIO debug console or Cortex Debug extension in VSCode. SWO/ITM trace for printf without UART: add `debug_extra_cmds = monitor tpiu config internal itm.log uart off 168000000`.
When should I use `-O0` (no optimization) with PlatformIO and arduino framework?

`-O0` (no optimization) with arduino:

Use for: debugging — full symbol info, variables not optimized away

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O0
```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -O0

Flash usage impact: minimal. Debug experience: excellent — all variables visible.
When should I use `-O0` (no optimization) with PlatformIO and stm32cube framework?

`-O0` (no optimization) with stm32cube:

Use for: debugging — full symbol info, variables not optimized away

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O0
```
Note: stm32cube defaults to -O0 in debug, -Os in release. Use build_type = release for optimized builds.

Flash usage impact: minimal. Debug experience: excellent — all variables visible.
When should I use `-O0` (no optimization) with PlatformIO and espidf framework?

`-O0` (no optimization) with espidf:

Use for: debugging — full symbol info, variables not optimized away

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O0
```
Note: espidf uses -Og by default. Component-level flags in CMakeLists.txt override global settings.

Flash usage impact: minimal. Debug experience: excellent — all variables visible.
When should I use `-O1` (basic optimization) with PlatformIO and arduino framework?

`-O1` (basic optimization) with arduino:

Use for: balanced — good for debugging with some optimization

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O1
```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -O1

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: good — most variables visible.
When should I use `-O1` (basic optimization) with PlatformIO and stm32cube framework?

`-O1` (basic optimization) with stm32cube:

Use for: balanced — good for debugging with some optimization

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O1
```
Note: stm32cube defaults to -O0 in debug, -Os in release. Use build_type = release for optimized builds.

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: good — most variables visible.
When should I use `-O1` (basic optimization) with PlatformIO and espidf framework?

`-O1` (basic optimization) with espidf:

Use for: balanced — good for debugging with some optimization

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O1
```
Note: espidf uses -Og by default. Component-level flags in CMakeLists.txt override global settings.

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: good — most variables visible.
When should I use `-O2` (full optimization) with PlatformIO and arduino framework?

`-O2` (full optimization) with arduino:

Use for: release builds — best speed without aggressive size changes

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O2
```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -O2

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.

Use for: release builds — best speed without aggressive size changes

In platformio.ini:
```ini

```
Note: stm32cube defaults to -O0 in debug, -Os in release. Use build_type = release for optimized builds.

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.

Flash usage impact: moderate. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.
When should I use `-Os` (size optimization) with PlatformIO and arduino framework?

`-Os` (size optimization) with arduino:

Use for: flash-constrained devices — minimizes code size

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -Os
```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -Os

Flash usage impact: reduced by 10-30%. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.

Flash usage impact: reduced by 10-30%. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.

When should I use `-O3` (aggressive optimization) with PlatformIO and arduino framework?

`-O3` (aggressive optimization) with arduino:

Use for: maximum speed — may increase code size, use with caution

In platformio.ini:
```ini
build_flags = -O3
```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -O3

Flash usage impact: may increase 5-15%. Debug experience: degraded — variables may be optimized away, use volatile for critical debug variables.

Use for: debugging — optimizes without interfering with debug info

In platformio.ini:
```ini

```
Note: arduino framework defaults to -Os. Override with build_unflags = -Os and build_flags = -Og

Flash usage impact: minimal. Debug experience: excellent — all variables visible.

How do I use ArduinoJson library with PlatformIO on esp32dev?

Using ArduinoJson on esp32dev (espressif32):

```ini
[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    bblanchon/ArduinoJson @ ^7.0.0
```

ArduinoJson works on all platforms. Use StaticJsonDocument for embedded (stack allocation, no fragmentation). DynamicJsonDocument for large payloads on ESP32 (more heap available).
How do I use PubSubClient (MQTT) library with PlatformIO on esp32dev?

Using PubSubClient (MQTT) on esp32dev (espressif32):

```ini
[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    knolleary/PubSubClient @ ^2.8
```

PubSubClient requires WiFi/Ethernet. ESP32 has built-in WiFi — include <WiFi.h>.
How do I use Adafruit_NeoPixel library with PlatformIO on esp32dev?

Using Adafruit_NeoPixel on esp32dev (espressif32):

```ini
[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    adafruit/Adafruit NeoPixel @ ^1.12.0
```

NeoPixel: Connect data pin to any GPIO. ESP32: use RMT peripheral (automatic via library). Any GPIO works.
How do I use TFT_eSPI library with PlatformIO on esp32dev?

Using TFT_eSPI on esp32dev (espressif32):

```ini
[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    bodmer/TFT_eSPI @ ^2.5.0
```

TFT_eSPI: Configure display in User_Setup.h (copy to lib/TFT_eSPI/). Set TFT_CS, TFT_DC, TFT_RST pins and display type (ILI9341, ST7789, etc.). ESP32: SPI at 40-80MHz, DMA enabled by default.
How do I use FreeRTOS library with PlatformIO on esp32dev?

Using FreeRTOS on esp32dev (espressif32):

```ini
[env:esp32dev]
platform = espressif32
board = esp32dev
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    
```

FreeRTOS: ESP-IDF includes FreeRTOS natively. Use xTaskCreate() directly.
How do I use ArduinoJson library with PlatformIO on blackpill_f411ce?

Using ArduinoJson on blackpill_f411ce (ststm32):

```ini
[env:blackpill_f411ce]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    bblanchon/ArduinoJson @ ^7.0.0
```

ArduinoJson works on all platforms. Use StaticJsonDocument for embedded (stack allocation, no fragmentation). DynamicJsonDocument for large payloads on ESP32 (more heap available).
How do I use PubSubClient (MQTT) library with PlatformIO on blackpill_f411ce?

Using PubSubClient (MQTT) on blackpill_f411ce (ststm32):

```ini
[env:blackpill_f411ce]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    knolleary/PubSubClient @ ^2.8
```

PubSubClient requires WiFi/Ethernet. STM32 needs external WiFi module (ESP-AT) or Ethernet (W5500).
How do I use Adafruit_NeoPixel library with PlatformIO on blackpill_f411ce?

Using Adafruit_NeoPixel on blackpill_f411ce (ststm32):

```ini
[env:blackpill_f411ce]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    adafruit/Adafruit NeoPixel @ ^1.12.0
```

NeoPixel: Connect data pin to any GPIO. STM32: uses DMA+timer for precise timing. Check pin compatibility with timer DMA.
How do I use TFT_eSPI library with PlatformIO on blackpill_f411ce?

Using TFT_eSPI on blackpill_f411ce (ststm32):

```ini
[env:blackpill_f411ce]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    bodmer/TFT_eSPI @ ^2.5.0
```

TFT_eSPI: Configure display in User_Setup.h (copy to lib/TFT_eSPI/). Set TFT_CS, TFT_DC, TFT_RST pins and display type (ILI9341, ST7789, etc.). STM32: SPI at up to 42MHz. Enable DMA for smooth rendering.
How do I use FreeRTOS library with PlatformIO on blackpill_f411ce?

Using FreeRTOS on blackpill_f411ce (ststm32):

```ini
[env:blackpill_f411ce]
platform = ststm32
board = blackpill_f411ce
framework = arduino
lib_deps =
    
```

FreeRTOS: Use stm32duino/STM32duino FreeRTOS library.

ArduinoJson works on all platforms. Use StaticJsonDocument for embedded (stack allocation, no fragmentation). DynamicJsonDocument for large payloads on ESP32 (more heap available).

```

PubSubClient requires WiFi/Ethernet. nRF52840 needs external WiFi or use BLE-to-MQTT gateway.

```

NeoPixel: Connect data pin to any GPIO. nRF52: uses PWM+DMA. Specify pin in constructor.

framework = arduino

    bodmer/TFT_eSPI @ ^2.5.0
```

TFT_eSPI: Configure display in User_Setup.h (copy to lib/TFT_eSPI/). Set TFT_CS, TFT_DC, TFT_RST pins and display type (ILI9341, ST7789, etc.). May require porting — check library compatibility.

framework = arduino

```

FreeRTOS: nRF52 SoftDevice includes FreeRTOS-compatible scheduler.
